repo_prior_art.cite: pid_synth_control_factor_misspec/v1 is scalar factor-misspecification partial ID; panel_sdid_mc_id/v1 is mean-estimator equivalence, not distributional rank-factor ID

published_prior_art.cite: Gunsilius2023DiSCo gives weighted Wasserstein/quantile synthetic controls, not this rank-invariant finite-donor compatibility boundary

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_synth_control_factor_misspec/v1 has scalar Chebyshev misspecification bounds, not open-set distributional rank escape

published_prior_art.cite: Chen2020DistributionalSC and FermanPinto2021ImperfectFit concern poor/imperfect fit, not exact-pre-fit nonidentification over rank-factor laws
Gunsilius2023DiSCo: weighted donor quantile curve in the stable convex-loading corner

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship: the proposal missed Chen-Dolado-Gonzalo 2021 Quantile Factor Models, a closer rank-factor prior; to reach flagship it needs an explicit regime-opening or strict-extension claim beyond that quantile-factor literature and DiSCo.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Chen-Dolado-Gonzalo 2021 Quantile Factor Models to Related work and explain precisely why fixed cross-rank unit loadings plus finite-donor causal identification is not covered by their quantile-factor estimation theory.

Upgrade the flagship claim from a row-span diagnostic plus tautological postconstant condition to a computable sharp identified-set formula with explicit lower/upper rank linear programs and a monotone-envelope condition.

If flagship is required, cite a published open problem or limitation statement showing that DiSCo or quantile-factor papers leave this larger rank-invariant finite-donor identification regime unresolved.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature comparison around Quantile Factor Models and sharpen the main boundary into a computable regime-opening result before asking for flagship acceptance.
